attributes.action: Reverse the freeze on Plan 2 student loan repayment thresholds.
attributes.additional_details: Freezing the Plan 2 repayment threshold at £29,385 from April 2027 for three years will increase repayments for millions of graduates. As wages rise, more people will be pulled into repayment and others will repay more each month. This effectively raises the cost of student loans without transparent debate and changes the real terms of borrowing after students have already graduated. The threshold should rise with inflation to ensure fairness and predictability.
attributes.background: We call on the Government to reverse the decision to freeze the Plan 2 student loan repayment threshold at £29,385 for three years from April 2027, and to restore annual uprating in line with inflation to protect fair, income-contingent repayment.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petition, which calls for similar action:

Review and reform Plan 2 student loan repayment terms
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/759316
